examples.title: Cartlane
examples.summary: A production-built commerce checkout proving Octane native form actions, pending state, idempotent server functions, SSR, and hydration.

journeys.title: Adopt a server-rendered product input, preserve its value, and add to the basket by keyboard
Retry the catalog, recover from an empty filter, and use the mobile basket offline
Reject a tampered cart, validate native checkout, and place an order through a server function
Queue rapid checkout submissions and resolve both to one charged order
Preserve checkout details through a payment decline and an offline retry

faultScenarios.description: An opt-in delay leaves the real server product form interactive before hydration so adoption and typed-value preservation are observable.
The scenario query fails one deterministic collection load and succeeds on explicit retry without changing the basket.
The local server function rejects the documented 4000 fixture card while preserving every uncontrolled native form field for retry.
A negative quantity is injected into the submitted cart; the server-owned catalog rejects it without an order or charge before a valid retry succeeds.
A disconnected browser retains the basket and checkout fields, reports the blocked action, and safely retries after reconnecting.
Two rapid native submissions cross the RPC boundary but share one idempotency key and resolve to one order and one charge.

examples.title: Cinebase
examples.summary: A streaming film and television catalog exercising Apollo cache isolation, SSR, hydration, and resilient navigation.

journeys.title: Filter the catalog and open a title with keyboard navigation
Keep the latest Apollo result when searches resolve out of order
Render empty and offline states and retry a failed GraphQL request
Add, reload, and remove a title from a device watchlist
Stream real server content and adopt input without losing user state

faultScenarios.description: The fixture transport lets both superseded searches complete; the moon query resolves after the harbor query so the visible Apollo result must remain isolated to the latest variables.
The outage query returns a deterministic GraphQL error until the user retries it.
An opt-in delay allows a user edit to server HTML before Octane adopts the input.

examples.title: Draftboard
examples.summary: A production-built SVG whiteboard proving Octane pointer capture, imperative refs, keyed identity, keyboard history, portals, and resilient autosave.

journeys.title: Load deterministic deep-linked boards and persist a first object from the empty canvas
Capture a high-frequency multi-object drag beyond the SVG while a keyed survivor retains identity
Draw a rectangle, zoom through the canvas handle, and pan the native SVG surface
Focus imperatively, undo and redo keyboard edits, and restore focus from portaled help
Retry deterministic failures while rapid offline edits converge to the latest saved board

faultScenarios.description: The load-failure scenario fails one deterministic board load and succeeds on explicit retry.
The save-failure scenario keeps edits visible through one failed autosave and saves the latest document on retry.
Native offline events queue completed canvas actions and reconnect saves the latest document.

examples.title: Flowboard
examples.summary: A production-built issue board proving Octane drag and drop, portaled routes, refs, keyed identity, and recovery behavior.

journeys.title: Load deterministic issues and recover from an empty filtered board
Open, deep-link, dismiss, and restore focus from a portaled issue dialog
Pointer-drag an issue while an unaffected keyed card retains DOM identity
Keyboard-drag across columns, retain keyed survivors, and restore focus with the native move control
Retry an initial fixture failure and apply rapid local moves while offline

faultScenarios.description: The scenario query fails the first deterministic load and succeeds on explicit retry.
The browser disconnects while several issue moves remain usable and visibly queued locally.

examples.title: Gridlab
examples.summary: A local-first virtualized spreadsheet proving Octane keyboard, clipboard, IME, formula, focus, and overlapping sync behavior.

journeys.title: Open deterministic formulas, restore an empty workbook, and keep the mobile grid usable
Scroll both virtual axes while selection focus survives and navigate to a distant cell
Copy and paste a native tabular selection without losing cell focus
Keep composition in the cell editor and recalculate committed formulas
Retry load and sync failures without dropping rapid edits across an offline interleaving

faultScenarios.description: The deterministic fixture fails once before a user-triggered retry opens the workbook.
Repeated and cross-cell changes remain local while the browser is offline and sync on reconnection.
The first save fails while a newer edit overlaps its in-flight snapshot; retry preserves the latest revision.
Out-of-range or malformed persisted cells are discarded before the deterministic sample workbook opens.

examples.title: Hacker News
examples.summary: One routed news reader implemented in both React-style TSX and TSRX over the same Octane data layer.

journeys.title: Browse distinct story feeds and follow story, comment, and user links
Show useful pending content before a delayed feed resolves
Keep the current page visible while delayed pagination resolves
Deliver styled server content and adopt it into an interactive client

faultScenarios.description: The fixture API delays feed responses so pending UI is observable.
Second-page item responses are delayed while the committed first page must remain usable.

examples.title: Harbor
examples.summary: A server-rendered React 19 pricing page hosting three compiled Octane islands through OctaneCompat — the React↔Octane boundary exercised end to end.

journeys.title: Preserve pre-hydration island input through adoption into the hydrated app
Drive island state natively and hand results back to the React shell
Flip React providers and observe islands re-render through shared contexts
Route an island fault to the React error boundary, keep siblings alive, and retry

faultScenarios.description: ?hydrateDelay caps a top-level await before hydrateRoot so pre-hydration interaction is observable.
?fault=recs makes the first recommendations refresh reject once so the island fault escapes to the React boundary.
Recommendation refreshes resolve after a fixed local delay so pending UI is observable.

examples.title: Lexical Playground
examples.summary: A production-built rich-text editor exercising the Octane Lexical binding through real browser interactions.

journeys.title: Type text, select it, and apply an accessible formatting toggle
Undo and redo visible editor content without losing the document
Open the portaled slash picker and insert a heading block

examples.title: Mailroom
examples.summary: A local-first email client proving real data-router fetchers, navigation blockers, durable drafts, and automatic offline outbox delivery.

journeys.title: Open a message deep link, navigate by keyboard, and persist a fetcher-owned star without navigating
Navigate every compact mailbox link by accessible name and keyboard without overflowing the mobile page
Block a dirty draft navigation, restore editor focus, save through a fetcher, and leave safely
Reset the editor across draft routes and update one adopted draft identity across repeated saves
Queue a message offline, retain it across route remounts, and deliver it automatically after reconnection
Retry a deterministic loader failure and distinguish empty and missing mailbox states
Keep a slow fetcher mutation alive across navigation and recover a rejected online send

faultScenarios.description: The fault=load query rejects the first inbox loader for a session and succeeds on explicit revalidation.
The fault=slow-mutation query keeps a shell-mounted shared fetcher active while the user navigates to another mailbox.
The fault=send query rejects the first online send for a session while retaining every draft field for explicit retry.
Native offline state stores a complete queued message locally, and the online event drives automatic fetcher delivery.

examples.title: Pagecraft
examples.summary: A resilient document workspace proving real Lexical selection, rich-text history, and versioned autosave across network races.

journeys.title: Open a document deep link, retain keyboard focus, and flush edits through immediate navigation
Format a live contenteditable selection and restore it through undo, redo, and reload
Retain save ownership across route remounts and settle versioned autosaves out of order
Retry a rejected save and persist an offline edit after reconnection
Reject malformed editor data, recover loading, and distinguish blank and missing documents

faultScenarios.description: The fault=load query rejects one deterministic document load and then succeeds from the retry control.
The fault=save query rejects the first debounced mutation while retaining the local editor state for an explicit retry.
A slow first mutation completes after a fast second mutation, and version checks preserve the newest serialized editor state.
Browser offline state pauses autosave while keeping the visible draft, then reconnect automatically persists it.
Lexical parses untrusted serialized state at both the save and load boundaries, rejecting unknown nodes without mounting corrupt content.

examples.title: Pulseboard
examples.summary: A production-built analytics console proving responsive Visx SVG measurement, accessible TanStack Table state, measured TanStack Virtual logs, keyboard navigation, and deterministic recovery.

journeys.title: Load a report deep link and preserve responsive, measured SVG geometry across navigation
Navigate the compact console and SVG points by keyboard while overlapping range requests converge
Sort, filter, select, empty, and restore an accessible TanStack campaign report
Window hundreds of measured activity rows and reveal a deterministic off-screen incident
Retry load and refresh failures without hiding retained data, then restore an empty segment

faultScenarios.description: The first deterministic dashboard load fails before report state is applied and succeeds on explicit retry.
The first snapshot refresh fails while the previous analytics remain usable, then succeeds on explicit retry.
The empty scenario renders a no-traffic segment that can restore the full deterministic sample.
Rapid 7-day and 30-day requests complete with different deterministic delays while only the latest choice is applied.

examples.title: Relay
examples.summary: A production-built team chat proving real SSE subscriptions, anchored history prepends, and duplicate-free reconnect replay.

journeys.title: Deep-link responsive channels and complete a portaled thread by keyboard
Receive and reconcile teammate and optimistic messages through the production SSE boundary
Preserve the visual anchor, focus, and survivor identity while rejecting stale cross-channel history
Replay missed updates once and settle rapid sends acknowledged out of order
Recover a failed history load without losing a draft and visit an intentional empty channel

faultScenarios.description: The fault=history URL fails its first deterministic history result and succeeds on retry without replacing the composer draft.
A completed general-channel pagination response is held across navigation and released only after the design channel is active, where it must be ignored.
Teammate events published while the EventSource is paused replay by sequence on reconnect and remain deduplicated.
A deliberately slow first send settles after a fast second send while final message order and uniqueness remain stable.

examples.title: Streambox
examples.summary: A production-built video platform proving Octane native media events, persistent player DOM state, and virtualized comments.

journeys.title: Play, pause, seek, change volume, and finish through native media events
Preserve the live video node through same-video routes and isolate engagement across videos
Window 180 comments, sort them, and recover from an empty search
Open a transcript deep link on mobile and control playback by keyboard
Retry a catalog failure and queue rapid actions while local playback stays offline

faultScenarios.description: The scenario query fails the first deterministic catalog load and succeeds on explicit retry.
The browser disconnects after media is ready; playback remains usable and product actions queue until reconnect.

examples.title: Threadline
examples.summary: A production-built social timeline exercising Zustand external state, optimistic mutations, and keyed survivor identity.

journeys.title: Navigate and follow from responsive deep-linked profiles, saved posts, and an empty profile
Preserve a survivor node, focused composer, and draft across keyed prepends
Roll back an optimistic post and concurrent likes, then retry every mutation after reconnecting
Settle rapid optimistic reactions acknowledged out of order
Retain a rapid optimistic publish across initial load failure and seed recovery

faultScenarios.description: The fault=initial-load URL fails once, preserves an optimistic publish, and merges it ahead of the seed after retry.
Posts and concurrent reactions update optimistically while offline, roll back visibly, and retain an independent retry action.
Two rapid reactions complete on deliberately different delays without clobbering one another.

examples.title: Wayfinder
examples.summary: A streaming travel planner proving Octane parallel use, out-of-order Suspense reveals, abort isolation, strict CSP, and hydration adoption.

journeys.title: Stream a real trip shell, reveal later weather before earlier fares, and complete a mutually gated parallel plan
Adopt a nonce-protected server trip and preserve a note typed before delayed hydration
Plan and save a city by keyboard, change its month, and restore it from the deep-linked shelf
Abort a slow destination search and keep a rapid trip switch on the latest route after stale work settles
Recover search and forecast failures, clear an empty result, and use the planner at mobile width

faultScenarios.description: The hydrateDelay query leaves real server trip content editable before hydration so DOM adoption and note preservation remain observable.
The later weather boundary resolves before the earlier fare boundary, producing a deterministic out-of-order stream without a live service.
Route and stay resources cannot complete unless both independent use creations start, turning parallel discovery into a functional result rather than a timing assertion.
The lo search is deliberately slower than ky; a native AbortController cancels it and late work cannot replace the current result.
The outage query fails once and returns a deterministic city after an explicit retry.
The weather-failure scenario streams an error arm once and succeeds through the public retry action.
